forecast-period.text: A ridge of high pressure will persist across the state. The high pressure centre will become established in the Tasman Sea over the weekend ahead of a cold front early next week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers mainly about the pastoral districts, possibly extending into the northern agricultural area. Possible morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures below average in the north, grading to near average in the south. Generally south to southeasterly winds, with local afternoon sea breezes.

Showers mainly about the pastoral and Flinders districts. Possible morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures below average in the north, grading to near average in the south. Generally south to southeasterly winds, with local afternoon sea breezes.

Possible showers mainly about the pastoral districts. Possible morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures below average in the north, grading to slightly above average in the south. Southeast to easterly winds, with local afternoon sea breezes.

text.p: Possible showers about the pastoral and western districts. Possible morning fog patches about southeastern districts. Maximum temperatures below average in the north, grading to above average in the south. Southeast to northeasterly winds, with local afternoon sea breezes.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Monday: less than 2 mm about the northern agricultural area and West Coast district, generally less than 5 mm about the Flinders and pastoral districts with local falls up to 10 mm.
